Inaugural Edition · July 2026

Indian Neuromuscular Summer School

Advancing
Neuromuscular
Excellence in India

A landmark advanced training programme bringing together India's foremost neurologists and world-renowned international faculty for an intensive week of learning, collaboration, and discovery.

About the Programme

A Historic First for the Indian Subcontinent

Inspired by the renowned Paris Summer School—which has been empowering specialists in neuromuscular expertise for three decades—the Indian NMSS is a unique and landmark opportunity for the region.

Carefully selected mid-level and senior neurologists from India and neighbouring countries—Sri Lanka, Myanmar, Kenya, Nepal, Laos, Vietnam and Iraq—will come together for 7 days of intensive lectures, case discussions and multiple parallel workshops from July 20–26, 2026, hosted under the aegis of the Neurology and Paediatrics Department of St. John's Medical College Hospital, Bengaluru.

40
Indian Neurologists
10+
International Participants
12
International Faculty
26+
National Faculty

Venue

Where It Happens

Bengaluru, Karnataka, India

St. John's National Academy of Health Sciences

One of India's premier medical institutions, St. John's Medical College Hospital provides the ideal backdrop for this milestone programme—combining world-class infrastructure with a legacy of excellence in medical education and research in the heart of Bengaluru.

The NMSS will be hosted under the aegis of the Neurology and Paediatrics Departments, with access to state-of-the-art lecture halls, simulation labs, and clinical facilities.

View on Google Maps

🏨 Accommodation

Accommodation has been arranged within the St. John's campus. 100 rooms are booked in Annexe 3, providing convenient, on-site lodging for all participants throughout the programme.

For booking details and allocation queries, please write to  info@neuromuscularsummerschool.com.

🏨 Nearby Hotels & Accommodation

A selection of hotels within easy reach of the St. John's campus. Participants are advised to book early.

For accommodation assistance write to info@neuromuscularsummerschool.com

The City

Bangalore — The Garden City of India

Called the Garden City of India, Bengaluru is renowned for its lush tree-lined avenues, pleasant climate, and vibrant intellectual culture. Every spring, the city bursts into spectacular bloom as Tabebuia Rosea trees—locally celebrated as Bangalore's "cherry blossoms"—carpet its boulevards in soft pink, turning roads like Jayanagar into enchanting canopies of colour.

Beyond its natural beauty, Bengaluru is India's Silicon Valley—a cosmopolitan metropolis brimming with culture, cuisine, and connectivity, making it the perfect host city for an international gathering of medical minds.

🌸 City of Gardens 🌡 Pleasant Year-Round Climate ✈ Well Connected Internationally 🍛 Rich Culinary Heritage 🏛 Premier Medical Hub

How Each Day Is Structured

Three Parallel Tracks Every Day

Each day of the NMSS runs three simultaneous tracks — giving participants a rich, multi-layered experience. The main school, hands-on workshops, and open symposia all happen in parallel.

🎓
Track 1

Main Programme

Intensive lectures, plenary sessions, and expert case discussions for selected NMSS participants. Covers the full breadth of neuromuscular disease — from clinical myology and neurophysiology to novel therapeutics and MDT care.

LecturesCase StudiesPlenaries
For: NMSS Participants only
+
🔬
Track 2

Parallel Workshops

Hands-on, skills-based workshops running simultaneously with the main programme. Topics rotate daily — muscle imaging, neurophysiology, genetics interpretation, respiratory care, and rehabilitation techniques.

Muscle MRI & UltrasoundEMG / NCSGeneticsRespiratoryRehab
For: NMSS Participants only
+
🌐
Track 3

Open Symposia

Seven standalone one-day symposia running in parallel — each a deep-dive into a focused topic, led by world-renowned faculty. Open to all registered delegates, not just NMSS participants.

DMD UpdatesSMA UpdatesTranslational ResearchNeurophysiologyPhysiotherapyRespiratory CareRehab & MDT
Open to: All Delegates
Symposia topics across the 7 days
Day 1
DMD MDT Care, Updates & Institutional Readiness
Day 2
Translational Research
Day 3
Physiotherapy Assessments & Interventions
Day 4
Neurophysiology
Day 5
SMA Updates & MDT Advocacy
Day 6
Rehabilitation in NMD
Day 7
Respiratory Care in Neuromuscular Disorders
